class="u-list-style-bullet">
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(x \in \{N,L\}\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(a \in \{N,R\}\)</span>, we reject the pair (refer to Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig15">15</a>).</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(z \in \{N,L\}\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(c \in \{N,R\}\)</span>, we reject the pair.</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y \in \{N,L\}\)</span>, we reject the pair unless <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> is a Q-node (which admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figg_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt="">). In fact, if <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> is not a Q-node, <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu _i})\)</span> contains at least a vertex different from <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and from <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span> that has to be placed on the spine to the right of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}^{i-1}(\mu )\)</span> and between <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span>. However, in a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}^{i-1}(\mu )\)</span> of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle x,y,z \rangle \)</span> with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y \in \{L,N\}\)</span>, there exists no portion of the spine between <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span> that is visible from the right page.</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(b \in \{N,R\}\)</span>, by a similar argument as in the previous case, we reject the pair unless <span class="mathjax-tex">\(i=2\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _1\)</span> is a Q-node (which admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figh_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">).</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul><p>Observe that after the above rejections either <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> or <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}^{i-1}(\mu )\)</span> are single edges, or all the conditions below hold:</p><ul class="u-list-style-bullet">
                    <li>
                      <p><span class="mathjax-tex">\(x = R\)</span> or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(a = L\)</span> (refer to Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig15">15</a>).</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p><span class="mathjax-tex">\(z = R\)</span> or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(c = L\)</span>.</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p><span class="mathjax-tex">\(y \notin \{N,L\}\)</span>.</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p><span class="mathjax-tex">\(b \notin \{N,R\}\)</span>.</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">Embedding Types for </span></b> <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}^i(\mu )\)</span>:</p><p>Regarding <i>p</i>: If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(x=a\)</span> (i.e., both are <i>L</i> or both are <i>R</i>) then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(p=x\)</span>, otherwise (either <span class="mathjax-tex">\(x=N\)</span>, or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(a=N\)</span>, or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(x=R\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(a=L\)</span>) <span class="mathjax-tex">\(p=N\)</span>.</p><p>Regarding <i>w</i>: If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(z=c\)</span> (i.e., both are <i>L</i> or both are <i>R</i>) then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(w=z\)</span>, otherwise (either <span class="mathjax-tex">\(z=N\)</span>, or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(c=N\)</span>, or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(z=R\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(c=L\)</span>) <span class="mathjax-tex">\(w=N\)</span>.</p><p>Regarding <i>q</i>:</p><ul class="u-list-style-bullet">
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y \in \{N,L\}\)</span> (and, thus, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> is a Q-node) then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(q=y\)</span>.</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y=R\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(b=L\)</span> then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(q=N\)</span>. If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y=R\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(b=B\)</span> then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(q=R\)</span>. If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y=R\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(b=R\)</span> (<span class="mathjax-tex">\(i=2\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _1\)</span> is a Q-node, which admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figi_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">) then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(q=R\)</span>.</p>
                    </li>
                    <li>
                      <p>If <span class="mathjax-tex">\(y=B\)</span> (observe that, since <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}^{i-1}(\mu )\)</span> is not a single edge, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(b \in \{L,B\}\)</span>) then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(q=b\)</span>.</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul><p>Since the set of rules above defines the set <span class="mathjax-tex">\(X_k\)</span> of embedding types realizable by <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}^k(\mu ) = {{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> and since the above computations can easily be performed in time linear in the number of children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>, we obtain Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar27">7</a>.</p><h4 class="c-article__sub-heading c-article__sub-heading--small" id="Sec13"><span class="c-article-section__title-number">6.5.2 </span>P-nodes: Variable Embedding</h4><p>Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node with poles <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span>, and with children <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _1, \dots , \mu _k\)</span>. A <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> is obtained by choosing a permutation for the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>, a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> for each child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> <span class="mathjax-tex">\((i = 1, \dots , k)\)</span>, and a bottom-to-top order of the vertices of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> whose restriction to each <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> corresponds to the bottom-to-top order of the vertices in <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span>; the embedding types that are realizable for <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> depend on the embedding types that are realizable for its children. Our approach to compute the realizable types of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> consists of considering one type at a time for <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>. For each embedding type, we check whether the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>, together with their realizable embedding types, can be arranged in a finite number of families of permutations (which we prove to be a constant number) so to yield a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of the considered embedding type. In order to ease the following description, consider that the arrangements of the children for obtaining some embedding types can be easily derived from the arrangements to obtain the horizontally-mirrored ones by (i) reversing the left-to-right sequence of the children in the construction and (ii) by taking, for each child, the horizontally-mirrored embedding type; for instance, the arrangements to construct a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figj_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> can be obtained from the ones to construct a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figk_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and vice versa. Moreover, two embedding types, namely Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figl_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figm_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt="">, are (horizontally) self-symmetric. As a consequence, in order to consider all the embedding types that are realizable by <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> we describe how to obtain only ten “relevant” embedding types (enclosed by a solid polygon in Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig12">12</a>): <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fign_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figo_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">,<img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figp_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">,<img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figq_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figr_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figs_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figt_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figu_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figv_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figw_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p>In this section, we give necessary and sufficient conditions under which the pertinent graph of a P-node admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figx_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. Then, we show how to test these conditions efficiently by exploiting a suitably defined flow network. The conditions for the remaining relevant embedding types, which can be tested similarly to Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figy_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, are reported in Appendix A.</p><div class="c-article-section__figure js-c-reading-companion-figures-item" data-test="figure" data-container-section="figure" id="figure-16" data-title="Fig. 16"><figure><figcaption><b id="Fig16" class="c-article-section__figure-caption" data-test="figure-caption-text">Fig. 16</b></figcaption><div class="c-article-section__figure-content"><div class="c-article-section__figure-item"><a class="c-article-section__figure-link" data-test="img-link" data-track="click" data-track-label="image" data-track-action="view figure" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y/figures/16" rel="nofollow"><picture><source type="image/webp" srcset="//media.springernature.com/lw685/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fig16_HTML.png?as=webp"><img aria-describedby="figure-16-desc" src="//media.springernature.com/lw685/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fig16_HTML.png" alt="Fig. 16" loading="lazy" width="685" height="248"></picture></a></div><div class="c-article-section__figure-description" data-test="bottom-caption" id="figure-16-desc"><p><b>a</b>-<b>b</b> Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> of Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar39">12</a> for a P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figz_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The spine is colored either green, blue, or black. The green part is the portion of the spine that is visible from the right, the black parts correspond to the bottom-to-top sequences of the vertices of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> inherited from the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBEs of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> (restricted to the vertices different from <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and from <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_{\mu }\)</span>), the blue parts join sequences inherited from different children. <b>c</b> Capacitated flow network <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> with edge demands corresponding to illustration <b>b</b></p></div></div><div class="u-text-right u-hide-print"><a class="c-article__pill-button" data-test="article-link" data-track="click" data-track-label="button" data-track-action="view figure" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y/figures/16" data-track-dest="link:Figure16 Full size image" aria-label="Full size image figure 16" rel="nofollow"><span>Full size image</span><svg width="16" height="16" focusable="false" role="img" aria-hidden="true" class="u-icon"><use xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="#icon-eds-i-chevron-right-small"></use></svg></a></div></figure></div><p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figaa_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""><b>.</b> Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node. We define the following two cases based on the existence of a child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figab_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">:</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> The children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> can be partitioned into two sets: The first set consists of a (possibly missing) Q-node child and a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figac_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The second set consists of any number (possibly zero) of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figad_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> The children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> can be partitioned into three sets: The first set consists either of a Q-node child, or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figae_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second set consists of any number (possibly zero) of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figaf_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of any positive number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figag_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figah_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one admitting only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figai_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                    <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar39">Lemma 12</h3>
                    <p>A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figaj_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> or Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> holds.</p>
                  
                    <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar40">Proof</h3>
                    <p>Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _1, \dots , \mu _k\)</span> be the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>. About the necessity, suppose that <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figak_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and denote by <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> the restriction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span>. At most one <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> can be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle N ,\cdot , \cdot \rangle \)</span> and all of them must be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,\cdot , R \rangle \)</span>. Therefore, all of the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> must be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle R ,R, R \rangle \)</span>, or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle N ,R, R \rangle \)</span> or <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,B, R \rangle \)</span>. It follows that either one of the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> has Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figal_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b>) or one has Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figam_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or none of the two (Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>). In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b>, there is nothing else to be proved for the necessity. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>, one of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> corresponds to a Q-node and has Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figan_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or it corresponds to a non-Q-node and has Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figao_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both because otherwise <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> would be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,B, R \rangle \)</span>; further one of the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma _i\)</span> must have Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figap_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figaq_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, because otherwise <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> would be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle R ,\cdot , R \rangle \)</span>.</p>
                    <p>For the sufficiency, we consider the two cases separately. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the possible Q-node child, if any, followed by the unique child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figar_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figas_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in any order; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig16">16</a>a. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the possible Q-node child, if any, followed by the possible non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figat_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figau_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in any order, followed by the possible child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figav_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, if any, followed by the children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figaw_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in any order, if any; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig16">16</a>b.</p>
                    <p>Assume that <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _1,\mu _2,\dots ,\mu _k\)</span> are ordered as defined above, according to the two cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>. We show that such an ordering of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> can be used to construct a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figax_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. For each <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle {\overline{\pi }}_i,\sigma _i \rangle \)</span> be a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu _i})\)</span> of the type of interest (i.e. the one considered in the description of the ordering); if <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _1\)</span> is a Q-node, then we consider <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle {\overline{\pi }}_1,\sigma _1 \rangle \)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figay_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, i.e., <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\sigma _1 \big ((s_\mu , t_\mu )\big )=L\)</span>. We denote by <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi _i\)</span> the restriction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\overline{\pi }}_i\)</span> to the vertices of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> different from <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and from <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span>. We now describe how to construct a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \pi ,\sigma \rangle \)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span>. In both cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>, the assignment <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\sigma \)</span> of the edges of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> to the two pages is the one determined by the assignments <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\sigma _i\)</span>, that is, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\sigma (e)=\sigma _i(e)\)</span> if <span class="mathjax-tex">\(e \in {{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu _i})\)</span>. The bottom-to-top ordering <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi \)</span> of the vertices of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> is constructed in a different way in the two cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> (see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig16">16</a>a) and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> (see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig16">16</a>b).</p>
                    <p>In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b>, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi \)</span> is constructed as <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu , \pi _1, \pi _2,\dots ,\pi _k, t_\mu \)</span>. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>, the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> whose <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBEs have Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figaz_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figba_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> are consecutive in the left-to-right sequence of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>. Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _p, \dots , \mu _q\)</span> be the subsequence formed by these children, with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2 \le p \le q \le k\)</span>. For each child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span>, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(i=p,\dots , q\)</span>, by the definition of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbb_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbc_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, there exist two consecutive vertices <span class="mathjax-tex">\(v'\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(v''\)</span> in <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi _i\)</span> such that the portion of the spine between them is visible from the left. Therefore, we can split <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi _i\)</span> into two subsequences <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi '_i\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi ''_i\)</span> where <span class="mathjax-tex">\(v' \in \pi '_i\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(v'' \in \pi ''_i\)</span>. Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu ^*\)</span> be the possible child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> whose <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE has Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbd_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">. Ordering <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi \)</span> is constructed as <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu , \pi '_p, \pi '_{p+1}, \dots , \pi '_q, \pi ^*, \pi ''_q, \pi ''_{q-1}, \dots , \pi ''_p, \pi _{q+1}, \dots \pi _k, t_\mu \)</span>, where <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\pi ^* = \emptyset \)</span> if <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu ^*\)</span> does not exist. In both Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> and Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \pi ,\sigma \rangle \)</span> is of a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbe_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                  <p>To test whether <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> admits a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbf_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, we show that deciding if one of Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> or Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> of Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar39">12</a> applies can be reduced to a flow problem on a network <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> with edge demands.</p><p>We describe the construction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> for Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> (refer to Figs. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig16">16</a>b and <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig16">16</a>c); the construction for Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> is similar. Network <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> is a capacitated flow network where each arc <i>e</i> has a label [<i>l</i>, <i>u</i>], where <i>l</i> and <i>u</i> are the lower and upper capacity of arc <i>e</i>. In particular, <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> contains a source <i>s</i>; a sink <i>t</i>; a node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> for each child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>; a node <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw494/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_IEq1782_HTML.gif" alt="">, representing each embedding type used in constructing the sequence of Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b>; and two special nodes <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\nu _1\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\nu _2\)</span>. Network <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> contains the following arcs: <span class="mathjax-tex">\((s,\mu _i)\)</span> with label [1, 1], for <span class="mathjax-tex">\(i = 1, \dots , k\)</span>; an arc <span class="mathjax-tex">\((\mu _i,\tau )\)</span> with label [0, 1], for each type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\tau \)</span> for which <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu _i})\)</span> admits a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\tau \)</span>; an arc <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw118/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_IEq1793_HTML.gif" style="width:118px;max-width:none;" alt=""> with label [0, 1]; an arc <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw118/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_IEq1794_HTML.gif" style="width:118px;max-width:none;" alt=""> with label [0, 1]; an arc <span class="mathjax-tex">\((\nu _1,t)\)</span> with label [1, 2]; an arc <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw113/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_IEq1796_HTML.gif" style="width:113px;max-width:none;" alt=""> with label <span class="mathjax-tex">\([0,\infty ]\)</span>; an arc <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw122/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_IEq1798_HTML.gif" style="width:122px;max-width:none;" alt=""> with label [0, 1]; an arc <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw121/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_IEq1799_HTML.gif" style="width:121px;max-width:none;" alt=""> with label <span class="mathjax-tex">\([0,\infty ]\)</span>; and an arc <span class="mathjax-tex">\((\nu _2,t)\)</span> with label <span class="mathjax-tex">\([1,\infty ]\)</span>. The size of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> is linear in the number <i>k</i> of children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>, because the outdegree of nodes <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> is bounded by the number of embedding types, <i>s</i> has outdegree <i>k</i>, and the remaining nodes have outdegree at most 1.</p><p>Clearly, a sequence corresponding to Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> exists if and only if <span class="mathjax-tex">\({\mathcal {N}}\)</span> admits a feasible flow, which has value <i>k</i>. Testing the existence of a feasible flow can be reduced in linear time to a max-flow problem [<a data-track="click" data-track-action="reference anchor" data-track-label="link" data-test="citation-ref" aria-label="Reference 74" title="Kleinberg, J.M., Tardos, É.: Algorithm design. Addison-Wesley, (2006)" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#ref-CR74" id="ref-link-section-d6319272e59481">74</a>]. We solve this problem by applying the standard max-flow algorithm by Ford-Fulkerson, which runs in <span class="mathjax-tex">\(O(|E({\mathcal {N}}) |\times f)\)</span>, where <i>f</i> is the value of the maximum flow. Since <span class="mathjax-tex">\(|E({\mathcal {N}}) |\in O(k)\)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(f=k\)</span>, deciding the existence of a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbg_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> takes <span class="mathjax-tex">\(O(k^2)\)</span>, which proves Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar28">8</a> for the case <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbh_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p></div></div></section><section data-title="Conclusion and Open Problems"><div class="c-article-section" id="Sec14-section"><h2 class="c-article-section__title js-section-title js-c-reading-companion-sections-item" id="Sec14"><span class="c-article-section__title-number">7 </span>Conclusion and Open Problems</h2><div class="c-article-section__content" id="Sec14-content"><p>Our results provide significant advances on the complexity of the <i>k</i> <span class="u-small-caps">UBE Testing</span> problem, i.e., deciding whether a directed graph admits a <i>k</i>-page book embedding. For <span class="mathjax-tex">\(k \ge 3\)</span> we showed that the problem is NP-hard; for <span class="mathjax-tex">\(k=2\)</span> we described polynomial-time and FPT algorithms for notable families of planar <i>st</i>-graphs. We conclude with some open problems. </p><ol class="u-list-style-none">
                <li>
                  <span class="u-custom-list-number">OP1.</span>
                  
                    <p>The digraphs in our NP-completeness proof are not upward planar. Since there are upward planar digraphs that do not admit a 3UBE [<a data-track="click" data-track-action="reference anchor" data-track-label="link" data-test="citation-ref" aria-label="Reference 72" title="Hung, L.T.Q.: A Planar Poset which Requires 4 Pages. PhD thesis, Institute of Computer Science, University of Wrocław, (1989)" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#ref-CR72" id="ref-link-section-d6319272e59791">72</a>], one can ask whether the  <i>k</i> <span class="u-small-caps">UBE Testing</span> problem remains NP-complete for <span class="mathjax-tex">\(k\ge 3\)</span> and upward planar digraphs. Observe that this question has been solved for <span class="mathjax-tex">\(k=2\)</span>, as it is known that <span class="u-small-caps">2UBE Testing</span> is NP-complete for planar <i>st</i>-graphs, which are upward planar [<a data-track="click" data-track-action="reference anchor" data-track-label="link" data-test="citation-ref" aria-label="Reference 42" title="Di Battista, G., Tamassia, R.: Algorithms for plane representations of acyclic digraphs. Theor. Comput. Sci. 61, 175–198 (1988)" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#ref-CR42" id="ref-link-section-d6319272e59855">42</a>].</p>
                  
                </li>
                <li>
                  <span class="u-custom-list-number">OP2.</span>
                  
                    <p>A natural extension of our work is to investigate other families of planar digraphs for which a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE always exists or for which polynomial-time testing algorithms can be devised.</p>
                  
                </li>
                <li>
                  <span class="u-custom-list-number">OP3.</span>
                  
                    <p>The complexity of our algorithm to test the existence of a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE for graphs with bounded branchwidth is dominated by the time complexity of computing a sphere-cut decomposition. This motivates the research of more efficient algorithms to compute a sphere-cut decomposition.</p>
                  
                </li>
              </ol></div></div></section>
                                </div>

In: Barequet, G., Wang, Y., (eds.) 35th International Symposium on Computational Geometry, SoCG 2019, June 18-21, 2019, Portland, Oregon, USA, volume 129 of LIPIcs, pages 13:1–13:22. Schloss Dagstuhl - Leibniz-Zentrum fuer Informatik, (2019)" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#ref-CR30" id="ref-link-section-d6319272e739">30</a>]. The current journal version significantly extends [<a data-track="click" data-track-action="reference anchor" data-track-label="link" data-test="citation-ref" aria-label="Reference 30" title="Binucci, C., Da Lozzo, G., Giacomo, E.D., Didimo, W., Mchedlidze, T., Patrignani, M.: Upward book embeddings of st-graphs. In: Barequet, G., Wang, Y., (eds.) 35th International Symposium on Computational Geometry, SoCG 2019, June 18-21, 2019, Portland, Oregon, USA, volume 129 of LIPIcs, pages 13:1–13:22. Schloss Dagstuhl - Leibniz-Zentrum fuer Informatik, (2019)" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#ref-CR30" id="ref-link-section-d6319272e742">30</a>], reporting all technical details, full proofs for all the results, and discussing several additional references</p></div></div></section><section aria-labelledby="appendices"><div class="c-article-section" id="appendices-section"><h2 class="c-article-section__title js-section-title js-c-reading-companion-sections-item" id="appendices">Appendix A P-nodes: Variable Embedding - Missing Cases from Subsection 6.5.2
                        </h2><div class="c-article-section__content" id="appendices-content"><h3 class="c-article__sub-heading u-visually-hidden" id="App1">Appendix A P-nodes: Variable Embedding - Missing Cases from Subsection <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="section anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Sec13">6.5.2</a>
                        </h3><p>In this section, we give necessary and sufficient conditions under which a P-node admits one of the relevant embedding types <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbi_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbj_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbk_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbl_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbm_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbn_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbo_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbp_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbq_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The conditions for <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbr_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> are discussed in Subsection <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="section anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Sec13">6.5.2</a>. The mentioned embedding types are enclosed by a solid polygon in Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig12">12</a>.</p><p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbs_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <b>Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbt_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <b>and Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbu_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar41">Lemma 13</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbv_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbw_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if all of its children admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbx_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar42">Proof</h3>
                  <p>For the necessity, let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> be any <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figby_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu ^*\)</span> be any child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> and let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma ^*\)</span> be the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu ^*})\)</span> obtained by restricting <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu ^*})\)</span>. Since <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> is of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figbz_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, there exist in <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma ^*\)</span>: (i) a portion of the spine incident to <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and between <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span> that is visible from the left page, and (ii) a portion of the spine incident to <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span> and between <span class="mathjax-tex">\(s_\mu \)</span> and <span class="mathjax-tex">\(t_\mu \)</span> that is visible from the right page. Thus, <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma ^*\)</span> has Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figca_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, i.e., <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu ^*\)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcb_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. For the sufficiency, we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> arbitrarily; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig17">17</a>a for an example. Clearly, the resulting <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> is of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcc_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                  <p><span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <div class="c-article-section__figure js-c-reading-companion-figures-item" data-test="figure" data-container-section="figure" id="figure-17" data-title="Fig. 17"><figure><figcaption><b href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y/figures/17" rel="nofollow"><picture><source type="image/webp" srcset="//media.springernature.com/lw685/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fig17_HTML.png?as=webp"><img aria-describedby="figure-17-desc" height="304"></picture></a></div><div class="c-article-section__figure-description" data-test="bottom-caption" id="figure-17-desc"><p>Constructions for P-nodes of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcd_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figce_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and <img xlink:href="#icon-eds-i-chevron-right-small"></use></svg></a></div></figure></div><p>The next lemma is proved similarly to Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar41">13</a>; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig17">17</a>b for an example.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar43">Lemma 14</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcg_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figch_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if all of its children admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figci_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar44">Lemma 15</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcj_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figck_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if it is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into two sets as follows: The first set consists either of a Q-node child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\omega \)</span> or of a child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\xi \)</span> that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcl_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both; the second set consists of any number, possibly zero, of children admitting Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcm_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar45">Proof</h3>
                  <p>The necessity is easy to see. In fact, for any child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>, the restriction of a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcn_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> to <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu _i})\)</span> is either of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figco_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcp_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. Also, a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcq_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> for <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> is incompatible with more than one <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcr_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> for its children, unless one of them is a single edge. If no child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is such that the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> is of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcs_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, then the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> would have Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figct_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                  <p>About the sufficiency: we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have: the Q-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\omega \)</span>, if any, with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcu_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\xi \)</span>, if any, followed by the remaining children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> in any order; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig17">17</a>c. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <p><b>Conditions for Type </b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcv_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""><b>.</b> Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node, we define two cases:</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.1</span></b> All children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admit either Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcw_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcx_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and there exist at least a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcy_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figcz_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.2</span></b> There exists a child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figda_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and all other children admit either Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdb_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdc_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar46">Lemma 16</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdd_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figde_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.1</span></b> or <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.2</span></b> holds.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar47">Proof</h3>
                  <p>It is easy to see that these conditions are necessary. In fact, for any child <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu _i\)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>, the restriction of a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdf_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> to <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu _i})\)</span> is clearly of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle X, B, R\rangle \)</span>, with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(X \in \{R,L,N\}\)</span>, since a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle S, B, T\rangle \)</span> implies that the embeddings of the <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBEs of the pertinent graphs of all children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> must be of type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle S', B, T'\rangle \)</span>, with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(S,S',T,T' \in \{R,L,N\}\)</span>.</p><div class="c-article-section__figure js-c-reading-companion-figures-item" data-test="figure" data-container-section="figure" id="figure-18" data-title="Fig. 18"><figure><figcaption><b id="Fig18" class="c-article-section__figure-caption"                   <p>For the sufficiency, both in Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.1</span></b> and in Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.2</span></b> we order the children from left to right in such a way that children with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBEs of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdh_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> precede all children with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBEs of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdi_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">; see Figs.<a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig18">18</a>a,<a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig18">18</a>b. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.2</span></b>, the child with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdj_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> is placed between the last child with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdk_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, if any, and the first child with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdl_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, if any; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig18">18</a>b. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdm_HTML.gif" <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admit either Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdn_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdo_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and there exist at least a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdp_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdq_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">RBN.2</span></b> There exists a child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdr_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and all other children admit either Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figds_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdt_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar48">Lemma 17</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdu_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdv_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RBN.1</span></b> or <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RBN.2</span></b> holds.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar49">Proof</h3>
                  <p>Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RBN.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RBN.2</span></b> are symmetric to Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.2</span></b>, i.e., they can be obtained from Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBR.2</span></b> after a vertical flip of the realizable embedding types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>. Thus, the necessity and sufficiency proofs are symmetric to those presented in Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar46">16</a>. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdw_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""><b>.</b> Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node. We define six cases:</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.1</span></b> There exists a child of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdx_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and the other children admit either Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdy_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figdz_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.2</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into three sets as follows. The first set consists of at least one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figea_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figeb_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figec_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The second set consists of any number, even zero, of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figed_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of at least one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figee_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figef_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figeg_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.3</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.2</span></b> by considering the types admitted by its children after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.4</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.6</span></b> These cases are obtained from Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.3</span></b>, respectively, by considering the horizontally-mirrored embedding types for the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span>.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar50">Lemma 18</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figeh_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figei_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if at least one of Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.6</span></b> holds.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar51">Proof</h3>
                  <p>Since Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figej_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> is self-symmetric, we have that, for each case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.3</span></b>, we can obtain a symmetric one (i.e., cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.4</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.6</span></b>) by reversing the left-to-right sequence of the children in the construction and by taking, for each child, the horizontally-mirrored embedding type. Thus, for each pair of symmetric cases, we only describe one of them.</p>
                  <p>For the sufficiency, we consider the three cases separately. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.1</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figek_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), in any order, followed by the unique child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figel_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figem_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), in any order; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig19">19</a>a. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.2</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the possible children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figen_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), in any order, followed by the possible child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figeo_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), followed by the possible children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figep_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), in any order, followed by the possible child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figeq_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), followed by the possible children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figer_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> (if any), in any order (see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig19">19</a>b). In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.3</span></b> we proceed as in Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NBN.2</span></b> by considering the types after a vertical flip. Clearly, these orderings allow for a <span class="mathjax-tex">\(2\)</span>UBE of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figes_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><div
