                  <p>The necessity can be argued by considering that in any 2UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figeu_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> the following hold: at most one of the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle N ,\cdot , \cdot \rangle \)</span>; at most one of the restriction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,\cdot , N \rangle \)</span>; and all the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> are of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,B, \cdot \rangle \)</span>. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figev_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""><b>.</b> Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node. We define two cases:</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">RRN.1</span></b> The children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> can be partitioned into two sets: The first set consists either of a Q-node child or of a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figew_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second set consists of any number, even zero, of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figex_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">RRN.2</span></b> The children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> can be partitioned into three sets: The first set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figey_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second part consists of any number, even zero, of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figez_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of at least one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfa_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfb_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfc_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar52">Lemma 19</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfd_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfe_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RRN.1</span></b> or Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RRN.2</span></b> holds.</p>
                
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar53">Proof</h3>
                  <p>Observe that Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RRN.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">RRN.2</span></b> are symmetric to Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> (see Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar39">12</a>), i.e., they can be obtained from Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.1</span></b> and <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRR.2</span></b> after a vertical flip of the types of the children. The necessity and sufficiency proofs are symmetric to those presented in Lemma <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="subsection anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#FPar39">12</a>. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figff_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""><b>.</b> Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node, we define six cases:</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.1</span></b> There exists a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfg_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""> and the other non-Q-node children admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfh_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.2</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into three sets as follows. The first set consists either of a Q-node child or of a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfi_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second set consists of any number, even zero, of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfj_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of at least one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfk_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfl_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfm_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.3</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.2</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.4</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into two sets as follows. The first set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfn_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second set consists of at least one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfo_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfp_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfq_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.5</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into three sets as follows. The first set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfr_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second set consists of any number, even zero, of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfs_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figft_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfu_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of at least one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfv_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfw_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">, with at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfx_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.6</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.5</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar54">Lemma 20</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfy_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figfz_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if one of Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.6</span></b> holds.</p>
                <div class="c-article-section__figure js-c-reading-companion-figures-item" data-test="figure" data-container-section="figure" id="figure-20" data-title="Fig. 20"><figure><figcaption><b id="Fig20" class="c-article-section__figure-caption" data-test="figure-caption-text">Fig. 20</b></figcaption><div class="c-article-section__figure-content"><div class="c-article-section__figure-item"><a class="c-article-section__figure-link" data-test="img-link" data-track="click" data-track-label="image" data-track-action="view figure" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y/figures/20" rel="nofollow"><picture><source type="image/webp" srcset="//media.springernature.com/lw685/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fig20_HTML.png?as=webp"><img aria-describedby="figure-20-desc" src="//media.springernature.com/lw685/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fig20_HTML.png" alt="Fig. 20" loading="lazy" width="685" height="236"></picture></a></div><div class="c-article-section__figure-description" data-test="bottom-caption" id="figure-20-desc"><p>Constructions for P-nodes of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figga_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""></p></div></div><div class="u-text-right u-hide-print"><a class="c-article__pill-button" data-test="article-link" data-track="click" data-track-label="button" data-track-action="view figure" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y/figures/20" data-track-dest="link:Figure20 Full size image" aria-label="Full size image figure 20" rel="nofollow"><span>Full size image</span><svg width="16" height="16" focusable="false" role="img" aria-hidden="true" class="u-icon"><use xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="#icon-eds-i-chevron-right-small"></use></svg></a></div></figure></div>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar55">Proof</h3>
                  <p>For the sufficiency, we consider the six cases separately. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.1</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Q-node child (if any), with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgb_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the unique Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgc_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgd_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig20">20</a>a.</p>
                  <p>In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.2</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Q-node child (if any), with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figge_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgf_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child (if any), followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgg_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the unique Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgh_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child (if any), followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgi_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order; see <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig20">20</a>b. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.3</span></b> we proceed as in Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.2</span></b> by considering the types after a vertical flip. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.4</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Q-node child (if any), with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgj_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgk_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child (if any), followed by the unique Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgl_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child (if any), followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgm_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig20">20</a>c. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.5</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Q-node child (if any), with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgn_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgo_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child (if any), followed by the unique Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgp_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child (if any), followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgq_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the unique Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgr_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child (if any), followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgs_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig20">20</a>d. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.6</span></b> we proceed as in Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NRN.5</span></b> by considering the types after a vertical flip.</p>
                  <p>The necessity can be argued by considering that in any 2UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgt_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""> we have the following: at most one of the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle N ,\cdot , \cdot \rangle \)</span>; at most one of the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,\cdot , N \rangle \)</span>; and that all the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> on the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> must be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,R, \cdot \rangle \)</span> or of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,B, \cdot \rangle \)</span>. However, not all the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> may be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,B, \cdot \rangle \)</span> as otherwise the resulting embeddings would be of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,B, \cdot \rangle \)</span>. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
                <p><b>Conditions for Type</b><img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgu_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt=""><b>.</b> Let <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> be a P-node. In order to describe when <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgv_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt="">, we need to define twenty-six cases. We assume that Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.i</span></b> applies only if Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.k</span></b>, with <span class="mathjax-tex">\(1 \le k \le i-1\)</span>, do not apply.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.1</span></b> There exist two children, one of which is a Q-node and the other admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgw_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.2</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into three sets as follows. The first set consists of a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgx_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt="">; the second set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgy_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The third set consists of any number, even zero, of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figgz_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.3</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into five sets as follows. The first set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figha_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both. The second set consists of any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighb_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighc_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The fourth set consists of any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighd_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The fifth set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighe_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.4</span></b> It is possible to partition the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> into four sets as follows. The first set consists of one child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighf_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The second set consists of any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighg_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighh_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The third set consists of at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighi_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt="">. The fourth set consists either of a Q-node child or of a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img both.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.5</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.4</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.6</span></b> There exists a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighk_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighl_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighm_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and at least one child that is either a Q-node or admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighn_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.7</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.6</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.8</span></b> There exists a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figho_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, at most one child that admits only Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw88/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighp_HTML.gif" style="width:88px;max-width:none;" alt="">, any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighq_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighr_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighs_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt="">. Furthermore, there exist either a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fight_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighu_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both; if only one of such children exists then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> has also a Q-node child.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.9</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.8</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.10</span></b> There exists a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw89/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighv_HTML.gif" style="width:89px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighw_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighx_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">. Furthermore, there exist either a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighy_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or a child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Fighz_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both; if only one of such children exists then <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> has also a Q-node child.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.11</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.10</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.12</span></b> There exists a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figia_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt="">, any number of children that admit Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figib_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> or Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figic_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt="">, and either a Q-node child or a non-Q-node child that admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figid_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt="">, or both.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.13</span></b> This case is obtained from Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.12</span></b> by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a vertical flip.</p><p><b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.14</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.26</span></b> These cases are obtained from Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.13</span></b>, respectively, by considering the types of the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> after a horizontal mirroring.</p>
                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar56">Lemma 21</h3>
                  <p>(Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figie_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt="">) A P-node <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> admits Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figif_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt=""> in the variable embedding setting if and only if one of Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.26</span></b> holds.</p>

                  <h3 class="c-article__sub-heading" id="FPar57">Proof</h3>
                  <p>Since Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figik_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt=""> is self-symmetric, we only need to consider the Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.1</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.13</span></b>; the Cases <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.14</span></b>–<b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.26</span></b> being analogous. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.1</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Q-node child, with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figil_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figim_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child; see Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig21">21</a>a. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.2</span></b> we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw90/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figin_HTML.gif" style="width:90px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figio_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figip_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child (if any), followed by Q-node child (if any), with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figiq_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt="">; see Fig <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig21">21</a>b. In Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.3</span></b> we proceed as follows. If there exists no Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figir_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child, we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Q-node child (which exists by the conditions of the case), with Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw83/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figis_HTML.gif" style="width:83px;max-width:none;" alt="">, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figit_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figiu_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figiv_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figiw_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child (which exists by the conditions of the case). See Fig. <a data-track="click" data-track-label="link" data-track-action="figure anchor" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y#Fig21">21</a>c. Otherwise (there exists one Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figix_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child), we order the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> so that from left to right we have the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figiy_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw87/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figiz_HTML.gif" style="width:87px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw91/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figja_HTML.gif" style="width:91px;max-width:none;" alt=""> child, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw86/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figjb_HTML.gif" style="width:86px;max-width:none;" alt=""> children (if any), in any order, followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figjc_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child (if any), followed by the Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw82/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figjd_HTML.gif" style="width:82px;max-width:none;" alt=""> Q-node child, if any. Subfigures (<b>a</b>) and (<b>b</b>) show the constructions for Case <b><span class="u-sans-serif">NNN.10</span></b> when there exists no Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw84/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figky_HTML.gif" style="width:84px;max-width:none;" alt=""> non-Q-node child and when there exists such a child, respectively</p></div></div><div class="u-text-right u-hide-print"><a class="c-article__pill-button" data-test="article-link" data-track="click" data-track-label="button" data-track-action="view figure" href="/article/10.1007/s00453-023-01142-y/figures/23" data-track-dest="link:Figure23 Full size image" aria-label="Full size image figure 23" rel="nofollow"><span>Full size image</span><svg width="16" height="16" focusable="false" role="img" aria-hidden="true" class="u-icon"><use xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="#icon-eds-i-chevron-right-small"></use></svg></a></div></figure></div>
                  <p>The necessity can be argued by considering that in any 2UBE <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> of <span class="mathjax-tex">\({{\,\textrm{pert}\,}}({\mu })\)</span> of Type <img src="//media.springernature.com/lw92/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1007%2Fs00453-023-01142-y/MediaObjects/453_2023_1142_Figkz_HTML.gif" style="width:92px;max-width:none;" alt=""> we have the following: at most one of the restriction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle N ,\cdot , \cdot \rangle \)</span> and at most one of the restrictions of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle \cdot ,\cdot , N \rangle \)</span> (notice that these two children may coincide in a single child of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle N ,\cdot , N \rangle \)</span>); at most one of the restriction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the non-Q-node children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle L, L, L \rangle \)</span> and at most one of the restriction of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\gamma \)</span> to the non-Q-node children of <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\mu \)</span> is of Type <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\langle R, R, R \rangle \)</span>. <span class="mathjax-tex">\(\square \)</span></p>
